<<<KOT:isogram>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"isogram (in its primary sense: a word where no letter occurs more than once) requires introspection into the written form of words themselves—specifically, the identity and frequency of graphemic tokens. This is a property of the written/symbolic representation of a word, not a property that can be grounded in the everyday sensory or bodily experience captured by the prime lexicon. It belongs to the domain of linguistic/orthographic analysis, not the kernel of perceptual and social primitives."}
<<<END:isogram>>>>

<<<KOT:katabolic>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"katabolic (or catabolic) is a technical biochemistry term referring to metabolic pathways that break down complex molecules. Its defining identity rests on enzyme-catalyzed chemical reactions at the molecular level—processes not observable in everyday experience and not expressible via the prime lexicon or available catalog concepts. It requires scientific knowledge of chemistry and cellular biology, not grounding in the perceptual or bodily primitives of the kernel."}
<<<END:katabolic>>>>

<<<KOT:laetrile>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"laetrile is a specific pharmaceutical compound with a defined chemical structure (derived from amygdalin). Its identity as a substance is determined by its molecular composition and pharmacological properties, not by everyday sensory or functional primitives. Moreover, it is a proper name for a particular drug (a historical medical artifact), and its significance is tied to technical medical and chemical claims. This belongs to the scientific/pharmaceutical domain, not the everyday kernel."}
<<<END:laetrile>>>>
